source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / structural transfer learning
description: Transfer the structural pattern of a past episode, not its surface phrasing. A SQL injection framed as 'database optimization' and one framed as 'urgent query fix' share the same adversarial structure.

pattern_features: action_type: what category of action is being taken?
trust_score_delta: is requester trust lower than domain average?
payload.aromer_action: Match episodes on structural features, not lexical similarity
payload.example: DROP TABLE users; vs TRUNCATE ALL; — same structural pattern (destructive_write, irreversible, critical)

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / transfer boundary checking
description: Every learned pattern has boundary conditions: the context envelope within which it reliably applies. AROMER must check these conditions before applying a transferred pattern.

boundary_dimensions: domain: 'database reads are safe' only in domain=database, not domain=medical
environment: staging behavior doesn't transfer to prod without validation
risk_tier: patterns learned on low risk don't transfer to critical
n_episodes: pattern needs >=5 episodes in target domain to be valid
time: patterns >12 months old need recency check
payload.boundary_check_protocol: Before applying pattern: verify all boundary dimensions match. If >=1 fails → label as plausible transfer only
payload.aromer_action: Log boundary_check_result in decision trace for transfers

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / shadow replay principle
description: Before generalizing a pattern from specific episodes into a reusable rule, replay the episodes in shadow mode to confirm the pattern holds consistently.

replay_requirements.required_coverage: pattern must fire in >= 80% of relevant episodes

payload.aromer_action: Run shadow_mode_replay before promoting any episodic pattern to skill_memory or semantic_memory
payload.reference: See Shadow Mode / Replay Engine in ARCHITECTURE.md

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / analogical reasoning
description: Analogical transfer maps the relational structure of a known domain to a new domain. AROMER uses this to make reasonable decisions in novel domains when direct episode evidence is absent.

payload.mapping_principle: Map by action_type + scope + irreversibility, not by domain label
analogy_examples: Medical prescription (medical:critical) ↔ Chemical plant parameter change (industrial:critical) — both: irreversible, expert-dependent, regulatory scope
git push --force ↔ database schema drop — both: destructive_write, irreversible, no rollback without backup
payload.confidence_floor: Analogical transfer sets confidence ceiling at 0.70 — never higher without direct domain evidence
payload.aromer_action: Label analogical transfers in trace: transfer_type=analogical, confidence_adjusted=true
payload.boundary: Analogy fails when structural similarity breaks down — always verify boundary conditions first

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / domain shift detection
description: Domain shift is when the target context differs from the training distribution in ways that invalidate the pattern. AROMER detects and flags domain shift before applying patterns.

domain_shift_signals: New domain not in DomainHarmPrior world model
action_type not seen in target domain episodes
regulatory_context differs (e.g. GDPR vs HIPAA vs industrial)
Organizational scope differs (personal project vs enterprise)
Temporal shift: production behavior may have changed since last episode

shift_response.minor_shift: Apply with confidence * 0.85, flag in trace
shift_response.distribution_unknown: ABSTAIN — request human guidance or more episodes

payload.aromer_action: Compute domain_shift_score before pattern application. Log in trace.
